channel.title: Programming on all okkio
channel.description: Recent content in Programming on all okkio

item.title: tidy evaluation 2
item.description: The first post is more explaatory and can be found here. This one is more a note-taking effort and hence much more be spartan. The main goal is to go through some of the functionality of the rlang package. Basics rlang has multiple constructor-functions that can be used instead of the base Rcombine operator c(). These are: lgl(...), int(...), dbl(...), cpl(...), chr(..., .encoding = NULL), bytes(...) and ll(...). The idea here is that this way the code is more explicit than using c() (The resulting data can be inferred from the functions).
